title: thea drupifera
intro: Thea drupifera, also known as the tea shrub or Assam tea, is a species of evergreen shrub native to the northeastern parts of the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. It is a major source of black tea and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Thea drupifera has various health benefits, including reducing the risk of heart disease, improving digestion, and aiding in weight loss, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Thea drupifera include tea infusions, extracts, and infusions blended with other herbs, which can be consumed hot or cold to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
